prompt: WebSocket messages are being broadcast to the sender as well. Also, private messages intended for one user are being sent to everyone in the room.

turns.content:
<task>
WebSocket messages are being broadcast to the sender as well. Also, private messages intended for one user are being sent to everyone in the room.
</task>

arguments.reasoning:
Found both bugs:
1. broadcast() (line 18-24): iterates all clients in the room but doesn't exclude the sender. Line 20 checks room but doesn't check `id !== senderId`.
2. sendPrivate() (line 26-30): sends to ALL clients instead of just the target. Should only send to the client with id === targetId.

arguments.summary:
Found both bugs in src/wsHandler.ts:
1. broadcast() line 20-21: sends to ALL clients in room including sender. Missing `id !== senderId` check.
2. sendPrivate() line 27-28: sends to ALL clients globally. Should only send to client matching targetId.

turns.content:
PASS  tests/wsHandler.test.ts
  WSHandler
    ✓ broadcast excludes sender (2 ms)
    ✓ broadcast only targets room members (1 ms)
    ✓ private message goes to target only (1 ms)
    ✓ private message to unknown user does nothing (1 ms)

Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests:       4 passed, 4 total

arguments.summary: All 4 tests pass. broadcast() now excludes sender, sendPrivate() now sends only to target client.
arguments.final_answer:
Fixed both WebSocket bugs in src/wsHandler.ts:
1. broadcast(): Added `id !== senderId` check so the sender doesn't receive their own broadcast.
2. sendPrivate(): Replaced iterate-all loop with direct Map lookup `this.clients.get(targetId)`. Now only the intended recipient receives the private message.
All 4 tests pass.
